request.question: Does CRISPR knockout of TP53 in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes increase doxorubicin sensitivity measured by LDH release?

hypothesis.measurable_outcome: doxorubicin sensitivity measured by LDH release

literature_qc.explanation: No retrieved papers discuss CRISPR knockout of TP53 in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes or measure doxorubicin sensitivity via LDH release. The papers focus on different topics such as organoids, cancer mechanisms, and cellular senescence, which do not align with the specific intervention or biological system of interest.

plan.objective: To determine the sensitivity of CRISPR knockout TP53 iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes to doxorubicin by measuring LDH release.
plan.experimental_design: This experiment will involve generating TP53 knockout iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes using CRISPR technology. The sensitivity to doxorubicin will be compared between the knockout cells and wild-type controls. LDH release will be quantified as a measure of cytotoxicity after treatment with varying concentrations of doxorubicin.
controls: Doxorubicin treatment of wild-type iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes at 1 µM for 24 hours, expected LDH release > 200 U/L.
Untreated wild-type iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes, expected LDH release < 50 U/L.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Cell Culture Preparation
Thaw iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes from liquid nitrogen storage by placing the vial in a 37°C water bath until thawed (~2-3 min). Transfer the contents to a 15 mL conical tube containing 10 mL of pre-warmed cardiomyocyte culture medium (RPMI 1640 supplemented with 10% FBS, 1% penicillin-streptomycin). Centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature. Discard supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 5 mL of fresh culture medium.
Equipment: 37°C water bath, Eppendorf 5810R centrifuge, 15 mL conical tubes
Reagents: RPMI 1640 (Gibco, 11875-093), Fetal Bovine Serum (FBS, Sigma, F7524), Penicillin-Streptomycin (Gibco, 15140-122)
Expected outcome: Cell viability > 90% as assessed by trypan blue exclusion.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Cell Culture Preparation
Count the cells using a hemocytometer and adjust the concentration to 1 × 10^6 cells/mL. Seed 2 mL of the cell suspension into each well of a 6-well plate (Corning, 3516) for a total of 12 wells. Incubate at 37°C with 5% CO2 for 24 hours to allow attachment.
Equipment: Hemocytometer, Corning 6-well plates, Incubator (Thermo Scientific, Heracell 150i)
Expected outcome: Cells adhere to the plate with > 80% confluency after 24 h.

DAY 1 | 30 min | CRISPR Design and Transfection
Design CRISPR guide RNA targeting TP53 using an online tool (e.g., Benchling). Order the gRNA and Cas9 protein from Integrated DNA Technologies (IDT). Prepare transfection mix by combining 1 µg of gRNA, 1 µg of Cas9, and 2 µL of Lipofectamine 3000 (Thermo Fisher, L3000015) in 100 µL of Opti-MEM (Gibco, 31985062). Incubate for 20 min at room temperature.
Equipment: Pipettes, Opti-MEM (Gibco, 31985062), Thermo Fisher Lipofectamine 3000
Expected outcome: Transfection mix is prepared and ready for application to cells.

DAY 1 | 15 min | CRISPR Design and Transfection
Remove culture medium from the 6-well plate containing iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes. Add 200 µL of the transfection mix to each well and gently swirl to mix. Incubate the cells at 37°C with 5% CO2 for 24 hours.
Equipment: Corning 6-well plates, Incubator (Thermo Scientific, Heracell 150i)
Expected outcome: Transfection is successful with > 70% cell viability after 24 h.

DAY 2 | 15 min | Selection and Expansion of Knockout Cells
After 24 hours of transfection, replace the medium with 2 mL of fresh cardiomyocyte culture medium containing 1 µg/mL puromycin (Sigma, P9620) to select for successfully transfected cells. Incubate for an additional 48 hours.
Equipment: Corning 6-well plates, Incubator (Thermo Scientific, Heracell 150i)
Reagents: Puromycin (Sigma, P9620)
Expected outcome: Cell survival indicates successful transfection; > 80% of cells should survive after puromycin selection.
step_by_step_protocol.linked_to: Selection and Expansion of Knockout Cells

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 4 | 30 min | Cell Expansion and Verification
After 48 hours of selection, replace the medium with fresh cardiomyocyte culture medium without puromycin. Expand the cells by splitting them into new 6-well plates at a 1:3 ratio. Incubate at 37°C with 5% CO2 for 72 hours.
Equipment: Corning 6-well plates, Incubator (Thermo Scientific, Heracell 150i)
Expected outcome: Cells reach > 80% confluency within 72 hours post-splitting.

DAY 7 | 1 h | Genomic DNA Extraction
Harvest cells by trypsinization using 0.25% Trypsin-EDTA (Gibco, 25200-056) for 5 min at 37°C. Inactivate trypsin by adding an equal volume of FBS. Centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature. Extract genomic DNA using the DNeasy Blood & Tissue Kit (Qiagen, 69504) according to the manufacturer's protocol.
Equipment: Eppendorf 5810R centrifuge, Trypsin-EDTA (Gibco, 25200-056), DNeasy Blood & Tissue Kit (Qiagen, 69504)
Expected outcome: Yield of genomic DNA > 1 µg per well.

DAY 7 | 1 h | Genotyping PCR
Set up a PCR reaction using 50 ng of genomic DNA, 0.5 µM forward and reverse primers targeting TP53, 1× Q5 Reaction Buffer (New England Biolabs, M0491S), and 0.5 U Q5 High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase (New England Biolabs, M0491S) in a total volume of 25 µL. Amplify using the following conditions: 98°C for 30 s, 35 cycles of 98°C for 10 s, 60°C for 30 s, 72°C for 30 s, and a final extension at 72°C for 2 min.
Equipment: Thermal Cycler (Bio-Rad, T100)
Reagents: Q5 High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase (New England Biolabs, M0491S), Q5 Reaction Buffer (New England Biolabs, M0491S)
Expected outcome: PCR products of expected size (e.g., 300 bp) for knockout confirmation.

DAY 7 | 1 h | Gel Electrophoresis
Prepare a 2% agarose gel in 1× TAE buffer (Thermo Fisher, BP1322) and load 5 µL of PCR products mixed with 1 µL of 6× loading dye (Thermo Fisher, 10616-024). Run the gel at 100 V for 30 min. Visualize bands using a UV transilluminator.
Equipment: Agarose gel electrophoresis apparatus (Bio-Rad, Mini-PROTEAN), UV transilluminator (Bio-Rad, Gel Doc XR+)
Expected outcome: Presence of bands corresponding to knockout alleles, confirming successful CRISPR editing.

DAY 8 | 30 min | Doxorubicin Treatment
Prepare doxorubicin stock solution at 1 mM in DMSO (Sigma, D8418). Dilute to final concentrations of 0, 0.1, 0.5, 1, and 5 µM in cardiomyocyte culture medium. Treat both TP53 knockout and wild-type cardiomyocytes with 2 mL of respective doxorubicin solutions for 24 hours at 37°C with 5% CO2.
Equipment: Pipettes, Incubator (Thermo Scientific, Heracell 150i)
Reagents: Doxorubicin (Sigma, D8418), DMSO (Sigma, D8418)
Expected outcome: Cells are treated with doxorubicin at specified concentrations without significant loss of viability prior to LDH assay.

DAY 9 | 1 h | LDH Release Assay
Collect 100 µL of supernatant from each well after doxorubicin treatment. Add 100 µL of LDH assay reagent (Promega, G1780) to each sample and incubate for 30 min at room temperature. Measure absorbance at 490 nm using a microplate reader (BioTek, Synergy H1) to quantify LDH release.
Equipment: BioTek Synergy H1 microplate reader, 96-well plates (Corning, 3590)
Reagents: LDH assay reagent (Promega, G1780)
Expected outcome: LDH release quantified with absorbance readings correlating to cell death.

DAY 9 | 1 h | Data Analysis
Calculate LDH release as a percentage of total LDH activity using the formula: (sample absorbance - blank absorbance) / (maximum LDH release - blank absorbance) × 100%. Compare LDH release between TP53 knockout and wild-type cells using a two-way ANOVA with a post-hoc Tukey's test for multiple comparisons.
Expected outcome: Statistical significance determined with p < 0.05 between groups.

DAY 10 | 1 h | Results Interpretation
Interpret the results based on the statistical analysis. Discuss the implications of TP53 knockout on doxorubicin sensitivity in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes and potential mechanisms involved in the observed effects.
Expected outcome: Clear conclusions drawn regarding the role of TP53 in doxorubicin sensitivity, with potential pathways identified for further investigation.

assumptions: TP53 knockout will lead to altered doxorubicin sensitivity in cardiomyocytes.
LDH release is a reliable indicator of cytotoxicity in this model.
The CRISPR system will effectively generate knockout cells with minimal off-target effects.
decision_criteria: Proceed if LDH release in TP53 knockout cells is significantly higher than wild-type controls at ≥ 1 µM doxorubicin.
Confirm successful knockout by genotyping with > 80% efficiency.
Evaluate cell viability post-treatment with > 70% survival rate.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same batch of iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes for all experiments.
Standardize doxorubicin treatment times and concentrations across all trials.
Maintain consistent culture conditions (temperature, CO2 levels) during cell growth and treatment.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: (numeric target TBD) improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.
